name: base_reposition
discovered: 2026-07-02, AlohaMini Pro grasp + real-InternDataEngine pick task

symptoms: IK/motion plan fails or converges with large position error although the grasp target itself is valid
planner reports 'did not converge' for EVERY sampled grasp pose of a reachable-looking object
arm stretches into a near-singular, contorted or self-overlapping configuration to reach
trace_evidence: target distance from the arm base exceeds the FK-sampled reach envelope (AlohaMini Pro: radial max ~0.425 m)
goal position in arm-base frame outside envelope bounds (e.g. y=+0.43 vs y-max +0.22)
kinematic teleport to the IK solution shows tiny TCP error, i.e. the solver is fine — the geometry is the problem

when_to_apply: The robot has a mobile base (even a position-held virtual one) and the manipulation target is outside or at the edge of the arm's comfortable envelope, or every IK/plan attempt from the current base pose fails.
repair_strategy: Do NOT keep tuning the arm. Sample candidate base stations around the target (grid or rotated approach angles a la ASPIRE), evaluate each by teleporting the base joints there and solving the manipulation IK, pick the best-scoring station, NAV the base there with the arm tucked, and only then run the manipulation. Separate NAV and MANIP phases explicitly.
code_sketch: for by in (0.10, 0.05, 0.01):            # stay outside obstacle footprints
nav_actions = [act(b, ARM_TUCKED, open) for b in interp(base_now, best.station, V_BASE)]
then run the validated manipulation recipe from the settled station
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro / ManiSkill AlohaMiniMultiYCB-v1: pick station ik_err 4e-4, place station 1e-3; pick->carry 15.5cm->place 18 mm error, first try
